original: Enid Blytonpublishes "Five on a Treasure Island" first of her "Famous Five" children's novels, start of one of the best-selling children's series ever with over 100 million sold
The Beatles record "Love Me Do", "PS I Love You", and a slow version of "Please, Please Me", with session player Andy White on drums; Ringo relegated to tambourine
Stromboli volcano, Sicily throws 2-ton basaltic rocks 2 miles
Hurricane Carla strikes near Port O'Connor, Texas with winds of 175 mph
US Surveyor 5 makes 1st chemical analysis of lunar material
Jingyi Le swims female world record 50m freestyle
The Atari 2600, originally known as the Atari Video Computer System (Atari VCS) is released in North America, revolutionizing the video game industry
XVII Summer Olympic Games close at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, Italy
Junxia Qu runs 1500m ladies' world record (3:50.46)
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthhits 50th home run during his MLB record 60 HR season in New York's 6-2 loss to St. Louis Browns at Yankee Stadium
Florence Chadwickbecomes the first woman to swim the English Channel from England to France, taking 16 hours and 19 minutes
Margaret Tynes, American child prodigy and international opera singer, born in Saluda, Virginia (d. 2024)
The French Blue gem (later the Hope Diamond) is stolen with other French crown jewels from Royal storehouse in Paris during Reign of Terror
German astronomer Max Wolf rediscovers Halley's comet
Founding of the World Wildlife Fund in Morges, Switzerland
Sri Lanka achieves its first Test cricket victory, defeating India by 149 runs
Lost steamship 'the Ship of Gold' SS Central America, sunk in 1857, rediscovered in waters off North Carolina by group led by Tommy Gregory Thompson using Bayesian search theory
Mahatma Gandhicoins the term "Satyagraha" to characterize the Non-Violence movement in South Africa
"TheCarol BurnettShow" starring Carol Burnett premieres on CBS-TV in the US
Gherman Stepanovich Titov, Soviet cosmonaut (Vostok II), born in Kosikhinsky District, Russia (d. 2000)
Battle of Lake Champlain: American navy defeats the Royal Navy at Plattsburgh, New York, ending the British invasion of the northern states during the War of 1812
Dedication of the first Temple of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in Europe, the Bern Switzerland Temple
Beatles' Magical Mystery Tour begins with a bus driven somewhat aimlessly around western England
Battle of Stirling Bridge, Scottish rebelWilliam Wallacedefeats the English
Philadelphia second basemanEddie Collinssteals MLB record 6 bases in the Athletics' 9-7 win over Detroit Tigers at Navin Field
Michelangelobegins sculpting the 12 Apostles for the Cathedral of Florence, over-life-sized marble statues of which only 1 was partly finished, that of St. Matthew
Johnny Miller becomes 1st New York Yankee to hit a HR on his 1st MLB at bat; his 2nd HR came on his final MLB at bat in 1969
American croonerBing Crosbyand British singerDavid Bowierecord duet "Peace on Earth/Little Drummer Boy" at ATV Elstree Studios near London for television special "Bing Crosby's Merrie Olde Christmas"
Elizabeth Daily, American actress (Pee-Wee's Big Adventure; The Powerpuff Girls - "Buttercup"), and pop singer ("Say It, Say It"), born in Los Angeles, California
Philip Ardagh, English children's books author (Eddie Dickens series), born in Kent, England
Physician Willem J. Kolff performs the first successful kidney dialysis using his artificial kidney machine in the Netherlands
The Battle of the Teutoburg Forest ends after four days with a Germanic alliance defeating Roman forces and putting an end to expansion of the empire east of the Rhine
ZR-1 (biggest active dirigible) flies over NY's tallest skyscraper, Woolworth Tower
First trans-Tasman crossing from Australia to New Zealand piloted by Charles Kingsford-Smith and Charles Ulm arrives in Christchurch after 14 hours 25 minutes
1st typesetting machine to dispense with metal type exhibited
Hurricane Iniki hits Kauai Hawaii; 3 die & 8,000 injured
Ögedei, son ofGenghis Khan, is elected the Mongolian Khan, succeeding Genghis after his father's death (or 13 Sept)
Father of the Blues,W. C. Handy, publishes his most famous composition, "St. Louis Blues"
Juan Manuel Fango in a Mercedes wins season ending Italian Grand Prix at Monza to take his third Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 17 points from EnglishmanStirling Moss
Ulisse Aldrovandi, Italian biologist and naturalist, created a museum and library of natural history, born in Bologna, Papal States (now Italy) (d. 1605)
Mungo Park, Scottish explorer (first westerner to travel the central Niger River in Africa), born in Selkirkshire, Scotland (d. 1806)
Pinto Colvig, American animation voice actor (Goofy, Pluto), born in Jacksonville, Oregon (d. 1967)
Robert Dixon Sr., American Baptist minister, and the last known survivor of the U.S. Army's all-Black "Buffalo Soldier" regiments, born in New York City (d. 2024)[1]
Arvo Pärt, Estonian composer (Für Alina; Fratres; Spiegel im Spiegel), born in Paide, Järva County, Estonia
Tommy Shaw, American rock guitarist, and singer (Styx - "Come Sail Away"; Damn Yankees), born in Montgomery, Alabama
Moby [Richard Hall], American musician (Play; 18), born in Harlem, New York
Taraji P. Henson, American singer and actress (Empire, Hidden Figures), born in Washington, D.C.
1st mobile long-distance car-to-car telephone conversation
Triple landslides bury Elm, Switzerland
Last German Q/pirate ship sinks near Easter Island / Rapa Nui
Battle of Brandywine, Pennysylvania; Americans lose to the British, Polish soldierCasimir Pulaskisaves life ofGeorge Washington
Begun on the 6th Sept, Americans defeat British at Battle of Plattsburgh during War of 1812
George Stibitz pioneers the first remote operation of a computer
US & Australian troops join in Salamaua, New Guinea
20.4 cm rainfall at Brunswick, Maine (state record)
Beatles' "Help!" album goes #1 & stays #1 for 9 weeks
Soyuz TM-22, lands
First true self-service supermarket, Clarence Saunders' "Piggly Wiggly," opens in Memphis, Tennessee
Stan Rowley, Australian athlete (Olympic gold 5,000m team, bronze 60m, 100m, 200m 1900), born in Young, Australia (d. 1924)
Jimmie Davis, American singer and songwriter ("You Are My Sunshine"), born in Quitman, Louisiana (d. 2000)
